Schema¶

Pydantic models for test suite definitions and run results.

Test suite¶

Pydantic v2 models for the YAML/JSON test suite schema.

The top-level entry point for users is load_suite(), which reads a .yaml, .yml, or .json file and returns a validated TestSuite instance.

Hierarchy:

TestSuite
├── suite: SuiteConfig
└── tests: list[TestCase]
            └── rubric: Rubric
                        └── criteria: list[Criterion]
class Criterion(*, name, description, weight)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single scoring criterion within a rubric.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Short identifier for the criterion (e.g. "empathy").

  • description (str) – Human-readable description of what the criterion measures.

  • weight (float) – Fractional contribution to the overall score. All weights in a Rubric must sum to 1.0.

name: str¶
description: str¶
weight: float¶
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class Rubric(*, criteria, passing_threshold)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Scoring rubric for a single test case.

Parameters:

  • criteria (list[Criterion]) – One or more Criterion objects. Names must be unique. Weights are automatically normalised to sum to 1.0 if they do not already; a UserWarning is emitted when normalisation is applied so that suite authors are aware.

  • passing_threshold (float) – Minimum weighted score (0.0–1.0) required for a test to be considered passing.

criteria: list[Criterion]¶
passing_threshold: float¶
validate_rubric_invariants()[source]¶

Enforce unique criterion names; normalise weights to sum to 1.0.

If the weights do not already sum to 1.0 (within 1e-6 tolerance) they are rescaled proportionally and a UserWarning is emitted. This keeps suite files forgiving while surfacing the discrepancy so authors can fix it intentionally.

Return type:

Self

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class TestCase(*, id, description, prompt, rubric, tags=<factory>, system_prompt=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

A single behavioral test case.

Parameters:

  • id (str) – Unique identifier for this test within the suite (e.g. "tone-empathy-001").

  • description (str) – One-sentence summary of what is being tested.

  • prompt (str) – The user message sent to the model under test.

  • rubric (Rubric) – Scoring rubric applied to the model’s response.

  • tags (list[str]) – Optional list of labels for filtering / grouping tests.

  • system_prompt (str | None) – Optional system prompt override. When omitted the model adapter’s default system prompt (if any) is used.

id: str¶
description: str¶
prompt: str¶
rubric: Rubric¶
tags: list[str]¶
system_prompt: str | None¶
classmethod tags_are_unique(v)[source]¶

Reject duplicate tags within a single test case.

Parameters:

v (list[str])

Return type:

list[str]

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class SuiteConfig(*, name, version, model, judge_model)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Suite-level metadata and defaults.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Human-readable name for the test suite.

  • version (str) – Semantic version string for the suite definition.

  • model (str) – Default model identifier used when running tests (e.g. "claude-sonnet-4-20250514").

  • judge_model (str) – Model used for LLM-as-judge scoring. May differ from model to keep judge costs predictable.

name: str¶
version: str¶
model: str¶
judge_model: str¶
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class TestSuite(*, suite, tests)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Top-level test suite definition, parsed from a YAML or JSON file.

Parameters:

  • suite (SuiteConfig) – Suite-level configuration metadata.

  • tests (list[TestCase]) – One or more TestCase objects. Test IDs must be unique.

suite: SuiteConfig¶
tests: list[TestCase]¶
test_ids_are_unique()[source]¶

Reject suites where two or more tests share the same ID.

Return type:

Self

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

load_suite(path)[source]¶

Load and validate a test suite from a YAML or JSON file.

Supports .yaml, .yml, and .json extensions. Raises specific exceptions so CLI error messages are actionable.

Parameters:

path (str | Path) – Filesystem path to the test suite definition file.

Returns:

A fully validated TestSuite instance.

Raises:

  • SchemaValidationError – If the file cannot be read, fails to parse, or does not satisfy the schema constraints.

  • ConfigurationError – If the file extension is not .yaml, .yml, or .json.

Return type:

TestSuite

Results¶

Pydantic v2 models for test run results and scores.

These models represent the output side of the evaluation pipeline. The runner populates TestResult with raw model output; the judge fills in CriterionScore objects and computes the weighted score. SuiteRun aggregates a full execution pass over a test suite.

Hierarchy:

SuiteRun
└── results: list[TestResult]
             └── criterion_scores: list[CriterionScore]
class CriterionScore(*, name, score, reasoning)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Judge-assigned score for a single rubric criterion.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – Must match a name in the corresponding Rubric.

  • score (float) – Normalised score from 0.0 (completely fails the criterion) to 1.0 (fully satisfies it).

  • reasoning (str) – One- or two-sentence rationale from the judge explaining why this score was assigned.

name: str¶
score: float¶
reasoning: str¶
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class TestResult(*, test_id, prompt, model, raw_output, criterion_scores=<factory>, weighted_score=0.0, passed=False, error=None)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Result for a single test case execution.

The runner sets test_id, prompt, model, and raw_output (or error). The judge subsequently populates criterion_scores, weighted_score, and passed.

Parameters:

  • test_id (str) – Matches id.

  • prompt (str) – The prompt that was sent (denormalised for self-contained storage and display).

  • model (str) – The model identifier that produced raw_output.

  • raw_output (str) – Verbatim text response from the model. Empty string if error is set.

  • criterion_scores (list[CriterionScore]) – One score per rubric criterion, in rubric order.

  • weighted_score (float) – sum(c.weight * s.score) over all criteria, as computed by the judge. Range [0.0, 1.0].

  • passed (bool) – True when weighted_score >= the rubric’s passing_threshold.

  • error (str | None) – Human-readable error description if the model call or judge scoring failed. None on success.

test_id: str¶
prompt: str¶
model: str¶
raw_output: str¶
criterion_scores: list[CriterionScore]¶
weighted_score: float¶
passed: bool¶
error: str | None¶
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class SuiteRun(*, run_id=<factory>, suite_name, suite_version, model, judge_model, started_at=<factory>, completed_at=None, results=<factory>)[source]¶

Bases: BaseModel

Aggregated record of a complete test suite execution.

Created by the runner at the start of a run, then updated as results arrive. Persisted to storage once completed_at is set.

Parameters:

  • run_id (str) – UUID string, auto-generated on construction.

  • suite_name (str) – Copied from name.

  • suite_version (str) – Copied from version.

  • model (str) – Model under test (may differ from suite default if overridden via CLI).

  • judge_model (str) – Model used for LLM-as-judge scoring.

  • started_at (datetime) – UTC timestamp set when the run begins.

  • completed_at (datetime | None) – UTC timestamp set when all tests finish (or the run is aborted). None while the run is in progress.

  • results (list[TestResult]) – Ordered list of TestResult objects, one per test case.

run_id: str¶
suite_name: str¶
suite_version: str¶
model: str¶
judge_model: str¶
started_at: datetime¶
completed_at: datetime | None¶
results: list[TestResult]¶
completed_after_started()[source]¶

Ensure completed_at is not earlier than started_at.

Return type:

Self

property total_tests: int¶

Total number of test results recorded so far.

property passed_tests: int¶

Number of tests that passed (passed=True, no error).

property failed_tests: int¶

Number of tests that completed scoring but did not pass.

property errored_tests: int¶

Number of tests that could not be scored due to an error.

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}¶

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].
